regime: Risk-Off Reversal (TSMOM-20 Stopped Out, All Legs Flip Short)
summary: The two-day risk-on run ended, and it ended as a tech-led reversal. MNQ opened lower, broke its opening range up at 13:55 UTC then failed, and sold off through the day to close 30,064.5 (-1.43%), losing its 1hr trend stop and flipping SHORT. TSMOM-20, long and healthy 24 hours ago, was stopped out on a 2-close break of 30,242 and is now flat. MES held far better (-0.12% to 7,533) but told the same story: it printed a record 7,579, reversed, and closed below its own 7,574 stop, so the equity complex is now both-short on the 1hr. The commodity side offered no shelter: gold spiked to 4,159.5 and reversed to close 4,072.6 (a large outside-day, stop flipped short), and crude fell 2.8% to 68.08. It was an event day (soft ADP +98K, ISM beat 53.3 but down MoM) and a de-risk into Thursday's June jobs report and the holiday close, not a growth-scare cascade. All four contracts closed below their 1hr stops.

setups.name: TSMOM-20 1hr (FLAT, stopped out)
setups.entry: No position. The long was stopped out; being long-only the system stands aside on the short
setups.t1: Re-arm only on a 2-close reclaim of the 30,339 flipped stop
setups.bias: OUT_OF_HABITAT. Do not flip short into a thin, pre-holiday, event tape. Wait for a clean stop reclaim (long) or a defined setup; protect capital and composure into Thursday's jobs report.

setups.name: ES Alignment Watch (MES)
setups.entry: A loss of the 7,500 psych on hourly closes broadens the risk-off
setups.stop: Reclaim of the 7,574 stop repairs the long
setups.t1: Downside toward prior value if 7,500 breaks
setups.bias: MES held 7,500 all day but flipped its stop short. 7,500 is the equity line in the sand: holding it keeps this a tech-led pullback, losing it deepens the both-short read.

event.name: ADP Employment (Jun) 8:15 ET and ISM Manufacturing (Jun) 10:00 ET
event.actual: ADP +98K (soft); ISM Manufacturing 53.3 (beat, but down from 54.0, 20th month of expansion)
event.surprise: Mixed: softer labor, firmer factory activity. The combination trimmed near-term easing odds while flagging a cooling jobs market, two days into a vertical run and one day before the June jobs report.
event.nqReaction: MNQ faded from the open, broke its opening range up at 13:55 UTC then failed, and sold off 1.4% led by mega-cap tech to close 30,064.5, below its flipped 1hr stop.
event.crossAsset: MES -0.12% to 7,533 (held 7,500 but flipped short). MGC spiked to 4,159.5 then reversed to 4,072.6 (+0.55% vs prior close, outside-day, stop flipped short). MCL -2.80% to 68.08. A de-risk across assets, not a flight to quality.

observe.name: GAP_NQ (gap fade)
observe.note: No gap-fade edge in a one-directional liquidation day; watch Thursday's post-jobs gap.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ (long-only 252)
observe.note: Longer-horizon long intact above its base, but a second distribution day and the short-horizon flip short warrant closer watch.

observe.name: ES confirmation
observe.note: MES held 7,500 on every hourly close but flipped its own stop short after a failed record high; the both-aligned long confirmation is gone.

risk.throttleState: DEFENSIVE. TSMOM-20 is flat (stopped out, stop flipped short) and being long-only it stands aside. No in-habitat trend to size into.
risk.ddProximity: Not the binding issue today; the position is flat. The real risk is behavioral, not a gap to a stop.
risk.dailyLossBudget: Intact. The binding risk is revenge-flipping short into a thin, pre-holiday, event tape.
risk.sizing: No new risk without a defined setup. Do not flip short reflexively; treat the 8:30 ET jobs print Thursday as an event open where the first move is suspect, and do not carry directional size into the Friday holiday close. The long-only equity stack takes neither the gold nor the crude short.
risk.note: Week ahead (holiday-shortened): Thu Jul 2 June jobs report (NFP) 8:30 ET then a 1:00 pm ET equity early close; Fri Jul 3 market CLOSED (Independence Day observed; Jul 4 Saturday). Next full session is Monday Jul 6.

psychology: The hardest trade after a stop-out is no trade. Two green days built a long bias, and when it reversed the instinct is to win it back fast by flipping short with the same conviction. That is revenge trading in a macro costume. The stop did its job: it took you out at 30,242 and the system is flat by design. You do not owe the market a position. On a thin, pre-holiday tape one day before the jobs report, the professional move is to let the fresh downtrend prove itself with a clean setup rather than chase the first red candle. Protecting capital and composure into Thursday is the edge tonight.

fullReport.title: Market Regime Snapshot (6-Axis)
fullReport.html:
AxisStateConfidence
DirectionRISK-OFF reversal. MNQ -1.43% to 30,064.5 closed BELOW a FLIPPED-SHORT 30,339 stop (buffer -275). MES -0.12% to 7,533 also flipped short after printing a record 7,579 and reversing. NQ led ES lower; all four contracts closed below their 1hr stops.HIGH
VolatilityElevated, expanding on the turn. MNQ ATR(14) 110; a wide outside-style session (overnight high 30,556 to RTH low 30,009, about 547 pts).MODERATE
SessionFailed opening-range break. MNQ OR 30,087 to 30,261 broke UP at 13:55 (30,283) then failed and reversed below the OR low. A false break on an event open (ADP 8:15, ISM 10:00).HIGH
InventoryNet short / long liquidation. Overnight buyers near 30,556 trapped; sellers in control from midday with no VWAP reclaim.MODERATE
EventEVENT DAY (POST). ADP soft (+98K vs ~110K), ISM Manufacturing 53.3 a beat but down from 54.0. Ahead: Thu Jul 2 jobs 8:30 ET + 1pm ET early close; Fri Jul 3 CLOSED.HIGH
LiquidityThin, pre-holiday. Half-day Thursday, closed Friday. Reduced depth amplifies moves.MODERATE

Cross-asset: Risk appetite reversed and narrowed. NQ led lower while ES held (a rotation and de-risk, not a broad crash). Gold spiked to 4,159.5 and reversed (no clean safety bid); crude fell 2.8%. Risk reduction across assets ahead of Thursday's jobs print, not a flight to quality.

fullReport.title: NY Opening Range / COMPASS (MNQ)
fullReport.html: NY Opening Range / COMPASS (MNQ). OR (13:30 to 13:44 UTC) 30,087 to 30,261, width 173 (wide, event open). A break UP at 13:55 (30,283) FAILED: price reversed back through the OR and closed 30,064.5, below the OR low. MES broke up at 13:55 and held better; gold and crude also saw failed upside breaks. The failed break was the COMPASS tell of an event-open trap, exactly the setup the filter flags on ADP/ISM mornings.

fullReport.title: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter)
fullReport.html: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter). Long invalidated. Price lost AVWAP, printed lower highs, and broke the opening range down after a failed up-break. The 1hr trend stop flipped short. Fresh long signals require a 2-close reclaim of the 30,339 stop, not a first touch.

fullReport.title: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ)
fullReport.html: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ). Center 30,172, sigma 65. Bands: +1s 30,238, +2s 30,303, +3s 30,368; -1s 30,107, -2s 30,041, -3s 29,976. Price closed 30,064.5, below AVWAP in the -1s zone, extended lower but not stretched to a reversion trigger. A long-side fade re-qualifies only on a -2s/-3s tag with an exhaustion bar and a center reclaim, half size. Bands are price-based this run (volume unavailable; Alphatrends indicator not loaded).

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked (Jun 30 map, checked on the Jul 1 session)
fullReport.html:
#Map rowResultMeasured
1MNQ holds above the 30,500 psych and the 30,484 AVWAP on hourly closesNOT TRIGGEREDNever held. Overnight closes slipped below 30,500 by hk49 (30,453) and the RTH opened 30,282.5, already under both. No continuation setup formed.
2MNQ loses the 30,242 ATR stop on a 2-close basisTRIGGERED, PLAYED OUTTwo consecutive hourly closes below 30,242 (30,190 then 30,163) confirmed it. TSMOM-20 flipped flat, price fell to the 30,009 RTH low (tagging 30,000 psych) and the 1hr stop flipped short.
3MNQ chops between the 30,242 stop and the 30,500 psych on the Jul 1 openNOT TRIGGEREDNo opening range trade in that band. RTH opened 30,282.5 and broke straight down through 30,242; a trend-down, not a contained range.
4MES holds 7,500 on hourly closesTRIGGERED, MIXEDMES held 7,500 on every hourly close (RTH low 7,525.5) as mapped, but printed a record 7,579 and reversed to close 7,533 below its 7,574 stop (flipped short). The level held, the both-long alignment did not.
5MGC holds above its 4,031 stop while MCL stays below its 71.03 stopTRIGGERED, MIXEDCrude stayed below 71.03 and fell 2.8% to 68.08 as mapped. Gold did not cleanly hold 4,031 (overnight closes ~4,013), then spiked to 4,159.5 and reversed to 4,072.6, flipping its stop short. Whipsaw continued, but risk did not stay equity-led-up.

fullReport.title: Tomorrow's Map (for Thursday, July 2, 2026)
fullReport.html:
Map for Thursday, July 2, 2026. Filters: not Mon/Wed, so BIR, HOF, MID and BNC are all eligible by weekday. But treat the 8:30 ET June jobs report as an event open (first OR break suspect), and note the 1:00 pm ET equity early close truncates the afternoon (HOF dead zone 12:00 to 14:00 ET still applies). Calendar: Thu Jul 2 June jobs report (NFP) 8:30 ET, then 1:00 pm ET early close; Fri Jul 3 market CLOSED (Independence Day observed; Jul 4 Saturday). All levels from today's 1hr chart.

yesterdaysMapScore.note: Risk-off reversal. Row 2 (lose the 30,242 stop on 2 closes) TRIGGERED and PLAYED OUT: 2 closes below (30,190 then 30,163), price fell to 30,009 and the stop flipped short. Rows 4 and 5 TRIGGERED but MIXED: MES held 7,500 yet flipped its stop short after a record high; gold did not cleanly hold 4,031 and reversed off 4,159.5 while crude stayed heavy below 71.03. Rows 1 and 3 NOT TRIGGERED (no hold above 30,500, no open-range chop).
